tools.name: drive_list
tools.description: List Google Drive files the user can access, newest-first by modifiedTime. Optionally filter by a Drive `query` (the Drive v3 `q` syntax, e.g. "name contains 'invoice'") and/or scope to a single `folder_id` parent. Returns one row per file (id, name, mime_type, modified_time, size, web_view_link) — NOT the file body. Use drive_read for content.

query.description: Drive v3 `q` filter (e.g. "name contains 'report' and trashed = false"). When omitted, lists non-trashed files.

folder_id.description: Restrict the listing to direct children of this Drive folder id.

page_size.description: Maximum files to return. Backend default applies when omitted.

page_token.description: Opaque cursor returned by a prior call. Omit on the first page.

size.description: Byte size; null for Google-native files (Docs/Sheets) which report no size.

tools.name: drive_read
tools.description: Fetch the text content + metadata of a single Drive file by id. Google-native files (Docs/Sheets/Slides) are EXPORTED to a text format (Docs→text/plain, Sheets→text/csv) — pass `export_mime_type` to override. Binary/plain files are downloaded via alt=media and returned as UTF-8 text (callers should only read text files; binary returns best-effort decoded text).

export_mime_type.description: Override the export MIME type for Google-native files (e.g. 'text/csv', 'text/plain', 'text/markdown').

exported_as.description: The export MIME type used for Google-native files; null for direct-download files.

tools.name: drive_upload
tools.description: Create a new Drive file from text content via a multipart upload. Sets name + mime_type and (optionally) a parent `folder_id`. Returns the new file's id + metadata row. v1 is text-upload only (no resumable/binary uploads).

mime_type.description: MIME type to store the file as (e.g. 'text/plain', 'text/csv', 'text/markdown').

content.description: UTF-8 text body of the new file.

folder_id.description: Optional parent folder id. When omitted, the file lands in My Drive root.

tools.name: sheets_read
tools.description: Read a rectangular range of cell values from a Google Sheet via spreadsheets.values.get. Returns the resolved A1 range + a 2-D array of stringified cell values (row-major). Empty trailing cells/rows are omitted by the Sheets API.

range.description: The A1 range the API actually resolved.

tools.name: sheets_append
tools.description: Append rows of values AFTER the last row of a table in a Google Sheet via spreadsheets.values.append (valueInputOption=USER_ENTERED, so formulas/dates are parsed). Returns the updated range + counts. Does NOT overwrite existing rows.

range.description: A1 notation of the table to append to (e.g. 'Sheet1!A1').

values.description: Rows to append; each row is an array of cell values.

tools.name: sheets_update
tools.description: Overwrite a rectangular range of cells in a Google Sheet via spreadsheets.values.update (valueInputOption=USER_ENTERED). Writes the supplied 2-D values starting at the range's top-left cell. Returns the updated range + counts.

range.description: A1 notation anchoring the top-left of the write (e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:C3').

values.description: Rows to write; each row is an array of cell values.

tools.name: docs_read
tools.description: Fetch a Google Doc's title + flattened plain-text body via documents.get. The structured document model is collapsed to text (paragraph runs concatenated, newlines between paragraphs) for downstream summarisation/editing.

tools.name: docs_create
tools.description: Create a new Google Doc with a title and (optionally) an initial text body via documents.create followed by a documents.batchUpdate insertText. Returns the new document id + title.

body.description: Optional initial text body inserted at the top of the new document.

tools.name: docs_update
tools.description: Insert text into an existing Google Doc via documents.batchUpdate (insertText). By default appends to the end of the body; pass `index` to insert at a specific 1-based character offset. Returns the document id + the number of batchUpdate replies applied.

index.description: Optional 1-based insertion offset. When omitted, text is appended at the end of the document body.

linked_sources.description: Google Drive v3 + Sheets v4 + Docs v1 — list/read/upload Drive files, read/append/update Sheet ranges, read/create/update Docs on the user's account. The Core requests THREE Google OAuth scopes under one grant: `drive` (Drive file read + create), `spreadsheets` (Sheets read/append/update), and `documents` (Docs read/create/update).

secrets.name: google_workspace_oauth
secrets.label: google_workspace
secrets.install_prompt: Connect your Google account so Neutron can read and create files in Drive (drive), read and write Sheets (spreadsheets), and read and edit Docs (documents).
